purpose: Proof-backed reference-app gallery index for existing Asupersync journeys, support-class labels, repro commands, structured-log expectations, and no-claim boundaries.

support_class_catalog.meaning: The journey has checked examples or tests in-tree and a focused repro command.
support_class_catalog.claim_boundary: This is reference coverage, not production support certification.

support_class_catalog.meaning: The journey has checked fixture projects or docs plus a contract lane.
support_class_catalog.claim_boundary: The fixture proves packaging or schema behavior only for its explicit surface.

support_class_catalog.meaning: The journey exists as a useful target but has a known implementation or infrastructure blocker.
support_class_catalog.claim_boundary: Blocked rows must render as blocked, never as passing reference apps.

structured_log_contract.failure_policy: missing required log fields or unredacted forbidden fields block the journey row

journeys.title: HTTP service plus database boundary
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_http_db_service CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test http_e2e --no-default-features -- --nocapture

RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_db_e2e CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_database --no-default-features -- --nocapture
no_claims: does not prove every HTTP route
does not prove live external database availability
does not prove production deployment readiness

journeys.title: WebSocket stream and conformance journey
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_websocket_stream CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_websocket --no-default-features -- --nocapture
no_claims: does not prove every RFC 6455 edge case
does not prove browser network stack behavior
does not prove production capacity

journeys.title: Background worker with graceful drain
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_background_drain CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_h1_graceful_drain --no-default-features -- --nocapture
no_claims: does not prove all supervisor topologies
does not prove OS signal delivery on every platform
does not prove broad shutdown correctness

journeys.title: Browser client and Rust consumer fixture
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_browser_client CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test wasm_rust_browser_example_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture
no_claims: does not prove browser GA readiness
does not prove every bundler or browser
does not prove live browser execution without the fixture scripts

journeys.title: Distributed and remote-runtime toy cluster
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_distributed_cluster CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_distributed --no-default-features -- --nocapture
no_claims: does not prove a production cluster manager
does not prove wide-area network behavior
does not prove distributed restore compatibility

journeys.title: ATP loopback transfer and CLI journey
repro_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_atp_loopback CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_tcp_loopback_e2e --no-default-features -- --nocapture
known_blockers: ATP CLI transfer surface still has blocked/fail-closed adapter work tracked separately
no_claims: does not prove cross-machine ATP file transfer
does not prove daemon delegation
does not turn blocked adapter status into passing reference coverage

proof_lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_app_gallery CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test reference_app_gallery_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

validation.rch_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_reference_app_gallery CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test reference_app_gallery_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

no_claims: does not create new production reference apps
does not run every journey
does not prove production deployment readiness
does not prove broad workspace health
does not prove release readiness
does not prove live RCH fleet availability
does not authorize local Cargo fallback
does not turn blocked adapter rows into passing coverage
